1	old_pattern	new_pattern	notes
2	GcRef::new(LuaTable::placeholder())	state.new_table()	already-existing helper
3	GcRef::new(LuaString::from_bytes(v))	state.new_string(&v)	or state.intern_str(&v) for short
4	GcRef::new(LuaString::placeholder())	state.new_string(b"")	placeholder = empty
5	GcRef::new(LuaProto::placeholder())	state.new_proto()	new D-1a method

7	GcRef::new(UpVal::closed(v))	state.new_upval_closed(v)	new D-1a method
8	GcRef::new(UpVal::open(thread_id, level))	state.new_upval_open(thread_id, level)	new D-1a method
9	GcRef::new(LuaUserData {...})	state.new_userdata(...)	method body still todo!; agent may need to implement OR leave site alone
10	GcRef::new(LuaCClosure {...})	state.new_c_closure(...)	method body still todo!; same caveat
11	LEAVE	LEAVE	If &mut LuaState is not in scope at the call site (e.g. inside LuaError constructors in lua-types/src/error.rs), DO NOT migrate. Tag with // TODO(D-1c-bridge) comment.

old_pattern	new_pattern	notes
GcRef::new(LuaTable::placeholder())	state.new_table()	already-existing helper
GcRef::new(LuaString::from_bytes(v))	state.new_string(&v)	or state.intern_str(&v) for short
GcRef::new(LuaString::placeholder())	state.new_string(b"")	placeholder = empty
GcRef::new(LuaProto::placeholder())	state.new_proto()	new D-1a method
GcRef::new(LuaLClosure { proto, upvals })	state.new_lclosure(proto, upvals.len())	new D-1a method; upvals filled with Nil
GcRef::new(UpVal::closed(v))	state.new_upval_closed(v)	new D-1a method
GcRef::new(UpVal::open(thread_id, level))	state.new_upval_open(thread_id, level)	new D-1a method
GcRef::new(LuaUserData {...})	state.new_userdata(...)	method body still todo!; agent may need to implement OR leave site alone
GcRef::new(LuaCClosure {...})	state.new_c_closure(...)	method body still todo!; same caveat
LEAVE	LEAVE	If &mut LuaState is not in scope at the call site (e.g. inside LuaError constructors in lua-types/src/error.rs), DO NOT migrate. Tag with // TODO(D-1c-bridge) comment.

content.content:
1035	    // C: checkHeader(&S);
1036	    check_header(&mut s)?;
1037	
1038	    // C: cl = luaF_newLclosure(L, loadByte(&S));
1039	    // loadByte(&S) reads the number of upvalues for the top-level closure.
1040	    let nupvalues = load_byte(&mut s)?;
1041	    // PORT NOTE: `luaF_newLclosure` allocates a closure with `nupvalues`
1042	    // upvalue slots.  In Rust Phase A we construct the struct directly; the
1043	    // GcRef wrapping happens after the proto is loaded.
1044	    // TODO(port): use the proper lfunc::new_lua_closure(state, nupvalues) API
1045	    // once lfunc.rs is translated and the API is settled.
1046	    let mut cl = LuaLClosure::placeholder();
1047	    cl.upvals = (0..nupvalues as usize)
1048	        .map(|_| std::cell::RefCell::new(GcRef::new(UpVal::closed(LuaValue::Nil))))
1049	        .collect();
1050	
1051	    // C: setclLvalue2s(L, L->top.p, cl);  luaD_inctop(L);
1052	    // macros.tsv: setclLvalue2s → state.set_at(o, LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::Lua(cl)))
1053	    // macros.tsv: luaD_inctop → (state.push already increments; use state.push)
1054	    // PORT NOTE: We push a placeholder Nil first; the real closure value is
1055	    // set after the proto is loaded.  This mirrors the C "anchor for GC"
1056	    // pattern.  In Phase A-C GC anchoring via the stack is not strictly
1057	    // necessary (Rc keeps things alive) but we preserve the stack discipline
1058	    // for behavioural parity.
1059	    // TODO(port): once GcRef<LuaLClosure> is cloneable into LuaValue, push
1060	    // the real value here instead of a placeholder.
1061	    s.state.push(LuaValue::Nil); // placeholder; replaced below
1062	
1063	    // C: cl->p = luaF_newproto(L);
1064	    let mut proto = LuaProto::placeholder();
1065	
1066	    // C: luaC_objbarrier(L, cl, cl->p);
1067	    // macros.tsv: luaC_objbarrier → state.gc().obj_barrier(p, o)  no-op Phase A
1068	
1069	    // C: loadFunction(&S, cl->p, NULL);
1070	    load_function(&mut s, &mut proto, None)?;
1071	
1072	    // Wrap the proto in a GcRef and attach it to the closure.
1073	    let proto_ref = GcRef::new(proto);
1074	
1075	    // C: lua_assert(cl->nupvalues == cl->p->sizeupvalues);
1076	    // macros.tsv: lua_assert → debug_assert!
1077	    // nupvalues is the byte we read; sizeupvalues = proto_ref.upvalues.len()
1078	    debug_assert_eq!(
1079	        nupvalues as usize,
1080	        proto_ref.upvalues.len(),
1081	        "upvalue count mismatch between closure header and prototype"
1082	    );
1083	
1084	    // C: luai_verifycode(L, cl->p);
1085	    // The macro is defined as `/* empty */` in the default build; dropped.
1086	
1087	    // Attach the loaded proto to the closure.
1088	    cl.proto = proto_ref;
1089	
1090	    // Wrap the closure in GcRef.
1091	    let cl_ref = GcRef::new(cl);
1092	
1093	    // Replace the stack placeholder with the real closure value.
1094	    // C: setclLvalue2s(L, L->top.p, cl)  — the slot we pushed Nil into
1095	    // macros.tsv: setclLvalue2s → state.set_at(o, LuaValue::Function(LuaClosure::Lua(...)))
1096	    // TODO(port): replace the placeholder at the correct stack slot.
1097	    // For now the top slot holds Nil; Phase B must fix this once
1098	    // GcRef<LuaLClosure> → LuaValue conversion is defined.
1099	    // TODO(port): update the stack slot pushed above with the real cl_ref value.
1100	
1101	    // C: return cl;
1102	    Ok(cl_ref)
1103	}

60	/// The value must fit in a VM register (u8).
61	pub(crate) const MAX_UPVAL: u8 = 255;
62	
63	// C: #define MAXMISS 10  (lfunc.h)
64	// macros.tsv: MAXMISS → const MAX_MISS: u32 = 10
65	/// Maximum consecutive misses before giving up the closure cache in `LuaProto`.
66	pub(crate) const MAX_MISS: u32 = 10;
67	
68	// ── Closure allocation ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
69	
70	/// Allocates a new C closure with `nupvals` upvalue slots, all initialised to
71	/// `LuaValue::Nil`.
72	///
73	/// The caller is responsible for setting the function pointer (`f`) and
74	/// populating the upvalue slots before exposing the closure to Lua code.
75	///
76	/// C: `CClosure *luaF_newCclosure(lua_State *L, int nupvals)`
77	pub(crate) fn new_c_closure(
78	    state: &mut LuaState,
79	    nupvals: u8,
80	) -> GcRef<crate::state::LuaClosure> {
81	    // C: GCObject *o = luaC_newobj(L, LUA_VCCL, sizeCclosure(nupvals));
82	    //    CClosure *c = gco2ccl(o);
83	    //    c->nupvalues = cast_byte(nupvals);
84	    //    return c;
85	    //
86	    // sizeCclosure is a C allocation-size helper; dropped — Vec handles sizing.
87	    // cast_byte(nupvals) → nupvals as u8 (already u8).
88	    // luaC_newobj → state.gc().new_obj(...) — in Phase A–C just Rc allocation.
89	    // gco2ccl → gc.cast_c_closure() — unnecessary in Rust, enum variant is the cast.
90	    //
91	    // TODO(port): LuaClosureC.f must be set by the caller. The C pattern allocates
92	    // then immediately assigns `c->f = fn`. Either make `f: Option<LuaCFunction>` in
93	    // LuaClosureC, or add a `new_c_closure(state, nupvals, f)` parameter. For now we
94	    // store a dummy; reconcile in Phase B.
95	    let closure = crate::state::LuaClosure::C(GcRef::new(LuaClosureC {
96	        // C: c->f is set by caller; placeholder index 0 here. LuaCFnPtr is a
97	        // registry index (see lua-types::closure); the caller overwrites it.
98	        func: DUMMY_C_FUNCTION_IDX,
99	        upvalues: vec![LuaValue::Nil; nupvals as usize],
100	    }));
101	    // C: luaC_newobj registers with the GC. In Phase A–C this is Rc::new.
102	    GcRef::new(closure)
103	}
104	
105	/// Allocates a new Lua closure with `nupvals` upvalue slots (all `None`).
106	///
107	/// The caller must set the `proto` field and populate `upvals` before the
108	/// closure is executed.
109	///
110	/// C: `LClosure *luaF_newLclosure(lua_State *L, int nupvals)`
111	pub(crate) fn new_lua_closure(
112	    state: &mut LuaState,
113	    nupvals: u8,
114	) -> GcRef<crate::state::LuaClosure> {
115	    // C: GCObject *o = luaC_newobj(L, LUA_VLCL, sizeLclosure(nupvals));
116	    //    LClosure *c = gco2lcl(o);
117	    //    c->p = NULL;
118	    //    c->nupvalues = cast_byte(nupvals);
119	    //    while (nupvals--) c->upvals[nupvals] = NULL;
120	    //    return c;
121	    //
122	    // sizeLclosure → dropped (Vec handles sizing).
123	    // c->p = NULL → proto field will be set by caller.
124	    // TODO(port): LuaClosureLua.proto is GcRef<LuaProto> (non-optional per types.tsv).
125	    // The C code allows NULL here (set later). Either use Option<GcRef<LuaProto>> in
126	    // the Rust struct, or require proto at construction time. Reconcile in Phase B.
127	    // For Phase A we use a sentinel value; this line will not compile as-is.
128	    let _ = state; // state used for GC registration in Phase D
129	    let _ = nupvals;
130	    // TODO(phase-b): LuaClosureLua.proto is non-optional; need a placeholder
131	    // until the caller assigns. Using LuaProto::placeholder() for Phase B compile.
132	    let lcl = GcRef::new(LuaClosureLua::placeholder());
133	    let closure = crate::state::LuaClosure::Lua(lcl);
134	    GcRef::new(closure)
135	}
136	
137	/// Fills a Lua closure's upvalue slots with freshly-allocated closed upvalues,
138	/// each holding `LuaValue::Nil`. Used when compiling closures that capture no
139	/// live stack variables.
140	///
141	/// C: `void luaF_initupvals(lua_State *L, LClosure *cl)`
142	pub(crate) fn init_upvals(state: &mut LuaState, cl: &GcRef<lua_types::LuaLClosure>) -> Result<(), LuaError> {

90	/// ```c
91	/// // C: typedef struct { lua_State *L; ZIO *Z; const char *name; } LoadState;
92	/// ```
93	///
94	/// PORT NOTE: In C, `LoadState` holds raw pointers to `lua_State` and `ZIO`.
95	/// In Rust these become references with a shared lifetime `'a`.  The struct is
96	/// always stack-allocated inside [`undump`] and never escapes the call.
97	struct LoadState<'a> {
98	    // C: lua_State *L;
99	    state: &'a mut LuaState,
100	    // C: ZIO *Z;
101	    z: &'a mut ZIO,
102	    // C: const char *name;  — chunk name for error messages
103	    // PORT NOTE: C uses const char * (a C string). In Rust we own a Vec<u8>
104	    // because the name slice may be a sub-slice of the caller's &[u8].
105	    name: Vec<u8>,
106	}

68	/// Build a `LuaError::Runtime` from a raw byte-string message.

72	fn runtime_bytes(msg: Vec<u8>) -> LuaError {
73	    LuaError::Runtime(lua_types::LuaValue::Str(lua_types::GcRef::new(
74	        lua_types::LuaString::from_bytes(msg),
75	    )))
76	}
77

content.text:
Output is `3`. Migration complete.

## D-1a Migration Report

**Sites migrated (6):**
- `func.rs:161` — `init_upvals`: `GcRef::new(UpVal::closed(Nil))` → `state.new_upval_closed(Nil)`
- `func.rs:198` — `new_open_upval`: `GcRef::new(UpVal::open(0, level))` → `state.new_upval_open(0, level)`
- `func.rs:598` — `new_proto` body collapsed to `state.new_proto()` (thin wrapper)
- `api.rs:1805` — `load`: hoisted to `let uv = state.new_upval_closed(gt); lcl.set_upval(0, uv);` to avoid borrow conflict with `set_upval`
- `undump.rs:732` — `load_locals`: replaced `unwrap_or_else` closure with `match` so `s.state.new_string(b"")?` can use `?` and the mutable borrow
- `undump.rs:1048` — `undump`: rewrote `.map(...).collect()` as a `for` loop so each iteration can take `s.state.new_upval_closed(Nil)` mutably

**Left as TODO(D-1c-bridge) (12):**
- `string.rs:43,778,850,909` — local rich types (`LuaStringImpl`, `LuaUserDataImpl`) that the state helpers don't yet produce, or free-fn context
- `debug.rs:73` — free fn `runtime_bytes`, no state in scope
- `api.rs:801,1192` — `state.new_userdata` / `state.new_c_closure` are still `todo!()`
- `state.rs:1624` — `push_closure` builds `LuaClosureLua` with pre-populated upvals; `state.new_lclosure` would clobber them with fresh Nil
- `state.rs:2754,2762` — `new_thread`: wraps a `LuaState` and a `LuaThread::placeholder`; no helper exists for either
- `state.rs:2967` — `new_state` free fn; LuaState not yet built
- `undump.rs:584,1073,1091` — wraps fully-populated `LuaProto`/`LuaLClosure` values rather than allocating placeholders

**Helper definitions (left untouched):** the `GcRef::new` calls inside `state.rs:1238/1258/1264/1569/1578/1583/1588`, `table.rs:1003`, and `func.rs:95/102/132/134` are the canonical allocation bodies themselves.

`cargo build -p lua-cli`: green. `target/debug/lua-rs 'print(1+2)'` prints `3`.
